campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and a detailed look at the treasurer's report. The council also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, including options for buses, biking, and walking.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
City treasurer's monthly financial report
New multimodal transportation plan to improve buses, bikes, and pedestrian access

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public meeting on March 18, 2026 where residents had a chance to speak about a proposed new rule or regulation. After hearing from the public, the council introduced an ordinance (a new city law) and decided to fast-track it for a vote at their next meeting instead of going through the normal multi-step approval process.
keyTopics: Public hearing held - residents could voice opinions
New ordinance introduced and expedited for approval
Fast-tracked for final vote at next regular council meeting
Skipped standard first reading requirement

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met to wrap up their 2026 weed removal program and approve new rules for how the council operates. They also voted to raise two flags celebrating Pride Month and Juneteenth, discussed affordable housing policy, and heard from the regional transit authority about transportation plans.
keyTopics: Completion of 2026 weed abatement program
New City Council operating procedures adopted
Affordable housing unit transfer policy
Pride Month and Juneteenth flag raisings approved
Housing element implementation schedule discussed

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council approved several contracts and agreements to move forward with construction projects and professional services. The council also voted to join a statewide insurance program (CJPIA) and discussed an art transfer agreement. Most of these items involved giving the Town Manager permission to finalize deals that had already been negotiated.
keyTopics: Construction contract with QLM, Inc. for a town project
Joining California Joint Powers Insurance Authority for better insurance rates
Professional services agreement with Dudek (likely for planning/consulting)
Multiple contract amendments with existing vendors
Art transfer agreement (details unclear from agenda)

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to review how well the city is serving its young people, including updates on youth programs, summer safety initiatives, and youth intervention services. The council also discussed code enforcement activities and plans to improve library facilities and services for the community.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and programs helping young people
Safe Summer Initiative to keep kids safe during summer break
Code Enforcement activities and enforcement efforts
Library facility improvements and customer experience updates

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that allowed residents to participate both in person and online. The main focus of the agenda information provided was explaining how residents could join the meeting virtually via Zoom or phone to ask questions or speak during public comment.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone dial-in
How to raise your hand to speak during public comment
Instructions for unmuting on phone to address the council

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held their regular meeting on April 9, 2026, where commissioners shared personal stories about their connections to the city. The group approved the minutes from their previous March meeting and conducted standard meeting procedures.
keyTopics: Personal stories shared by three commissioners about Sunnyvale
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Human Relations Commission regular business

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met on April 8, 2026 to discuss several community initiatives including a program called Budget and Bites, a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved minutes from their previous meeting and opened the floor for residents to speak about any local issues.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
City of Cupertino summer jobs for young people
Approval of March 11, 2026 meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met on April 7, 2026 to conduct regular business. The council is offering both in-person attendance and remote participation options so residents can watch and participate from home or in person.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom)
Remote participation via Zoom video conference
Phone dial-in option for listening to meetings
Written public comments can be submitted online before meetings

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve several administrative and financial matters, including joining a tax board partnership with the county, considering fee waivers for a community educational organization, and awarding a construction contract for a city project. The council also reviewed funding plan amendments and new requests from individual council members.
keyTopics: Tax Board partnership with county (Franchise Tax Board)
Fee waiver and donation requests for Milpitas Community Educational
Construction contract approval and project plans
Updates to city's 2025-2030 funding plan
New workplan requests from council members

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to approve the city's gas utility plans and rates for 2027. The main discussion involved reviewing the financial health of the gas utility, moving money between accounts as needed, and adjusting what residents and businesses will pay for natural gas service.
keyTopics: Gas utility rates are changing for residential and commercial customers in 2027
City is transferring money between the gas utility fund and general city budget
Gas utility financial forecast was reviewed for fiscal year 2027
Separate rate schedules approved for residential, commercial, and large commercial gas users
